docs.title: DV Debug Lab
docs.text:
Practical debug notes for SystemVerilog, UVM, GLS, CDC, X-propagation, assertions, coverage, and regression scripting.

This site is built for engineers who do not need another vague textbook explanation. They need the bug, the cause, the fix, and the checklist before the simulator invents a new way to ruin lunch.

docs.title: What you will find here
docs.text:
• SystemVerilog

    Blocking and non-blocking assignments, race conditions, clocking blocks, randomization failures, plusargs, and package/import debug.

  • UVM Debug

    Scoreboards, analysis ports, config DB, virtual interfaces, sequences, drivers, monitors, and phase objections.

  • GLS Debug

    SDF annotation, X propagation, timing violations, reset behavior, and why RTL passes while GLS behaves like a haunted netlist.

  • CDC & X-Propagation

    Synchronizers, pulse crossing, metastability, glitches, and gate-level-only clock-domain problems.

  • Assertions & Coverage

    SVA protocol checks, assertion failures, covergroups, bins, sampling issues, and practical closure workflows.

  • Regression & Scripting

    Shell scripts, Makefiles, plusargs, logs, grep, awk, sed, Vim, and automation workflows for verification teams.

  • Interview Prep

    Practical DV questions, debug scenarios, whiteboard flows, and concise explanations across SystemVerilog, UVM, CDC, GLS, and scripting.

docs.text:
• Start Here
  • Topic Index

docs.title: 30-day build target
docs.text:
The first content sprint will publish 30 practical topics in 30 days.

Each topic will include:

  • a full article
  • a minimal broken example
  • a corrected example
  • debug checklist
  • common error messages
  • interview angle
  • downloadable code folder

docs.title: Current status
docs.text: This is the Phase 0 website shell. Topic pages will be added daily.

docs.title: About DV Debug Lab
docs.text:
DV Debug Lab is a practical knowledge base for design verification engineers.

The goal is simple:

Turn painful verification-debug problems into clear articles, examples, and checklists.

This site focuses on:

  • SystemVerilog
  • UVM
  • gate-level simulation
  • CDC
  • X-propagation
  • assertions
  • functional coverage
  • regression scripting
  • interview preparation

docs.title: Philosophy
docs.text:
Most verification resources explain concepts. DV Debug Lab explains what to do when things break.

Because things will break. Silicon and simulators both have a flair for drama.

docs.title: Contact
docs.text: For now, use GitHub issues for corrections, suggestions, and topic requests.

docs.title: Suggested issue types
docs.text:
• Article correction
  • New topic request
  • Code example issue
  • Broken link
  • Premium pack request

Replace this page later with your email, LinkedIn, or contact form.

docs.title: Start Here
docs.text:
DV Debug Lab is organized around real verification-debug problems.

Use it like this:

  1. Search your symptom.
  2. Open the matching article.
  3. Compare the broken and fixed examples.
  4. Follow the debug checklist.
  5. Download or copy the example structure.
  6. Adapt it to your testbench.

docs.title: Best path for beginners
docs.text:
1. SystemVerilog
  2. UVM Debug
  3. Assertions & Coverage
  4. CDC & X-Propagation
  5. GLS Debug
  6. Regression & Scripting
  7. Interview Prep

docs.title: How articles are structured
docs.text:
Each article follows the same template:

  • Problem summary
  • Where this happens
  • Why it happens
  • Minimal broken example
  • Corrected example
  • Debug flow
  • Checklist
  • Common simulator messages
  • Interview angle
  • Downloadable files
  • Related topics

Repetition is not laziness here. It is navigation with manners.

docs.title: LinkedIn Post Template
docs.text:
Debug note of the day:

[One-line bug symptom]

Common causes:

  1. Cause one
  2. Cause two
  3. Cause three

Quick debug flow:

  • Step one
  • Step two
  • Step three

I wrote a full example with broken code, fixed code, and a checklist here:

[article link]

docs.title: Debug note: [Topic]
docs.text:
This week's bug:

[Problem statement]

Why it happens:

  • [Cause 1]
  • [Cause 2]
  • [Cause 3]

Quick fix:

  1. [Step 1]
  2. [Step 2]
  3. [Step 3]

Full article: [link] Example files: [link]

docs.title: Problem summary
docs.text:
Write the exact problem in one paragraph.

Example:

The monitor prints transactions, but the scoreboard does not receive anything through the analysis port.

docs.title: Where this happens
docs.text:
• UVM environments
  • Monitor-to-scoreboard connections
  • Analysis port/export/imp usage
  • Testbench bring-up

docs.title: Why it happens
docs.text:
Explain the root causes.

Common causes:

  1. Analysis port not connected
  2. Wrong analysis imp declaration
  3. Wrong transaction type
  4. Scoreboard not constructed
  5. Component hierarchy mismatch
  6. Phase ordering issue

docs.title: Minimal broken example
docs.text: // Broken code goes here

docs.title: Corrected example
docs.text: // Fixed code goes here

docs.title: Debug flow
docs.text:
1. Confirm monitor writes the transaction.
  2. Confirm scoreboard exists.
  3. Confirm connection happens in connect_phase.
  4. Confirm type matches.
  5. Confirm scoreboard write() is implemented.
  6. Add debug prints at each boundary.

docs.title: Checklist
docs.text:
• Monitor prints transaction before ap.write(tx)
  • Analysis port is constructed
  • Scoreboard is constructed
  • Port is connected in connect_phase
  • Transaction type matches
  • Scoreboard has correct write() signature
  • Factory did not replace the expected component accidentally

docs.title: Common simulator messages
docs.text: Add real error/warning messages here when available.

docs.title: Interview angle
docs.text:
A common interview version of this problem:

A UVM monitor is collecting packets, but the scoreboard is empty. How would you debug it?

Expected answer:

  • Check monitor write path
  • Check analysis connection
  • Check scoreboard implementation
  • Check transaction type
  • Check build/connect phase ordering

docs.title: Daily Upload Checklist
docs.text: Use this after creating a new topic package.

docs.title: Files to add
docs.text:
• Add article under the correct docs/ category
  • Add example folder under examples/
  • Add images/diagrams under docs/assets/
  • Update mkdocs.yml navigation
  • Update docs/topics.md
  • Update category index page if needed

docs.title: Local validation
docs.text:
mkdocs serve

Check:

  • Page opens
  • Navigation link works
  • Code blocks render correctly
  • Checklist renders correctly
  • Internal links work
  • No broken headings
  • No company-confidential information

docs.title: Publish
docs.text:
git add .
git commit -m "Add topic: short topic name"
git push
